design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore time periods, recruit party members, engage in visible-enemy turn-based combat with collaborative tech systems, progress narrative through character-driven side quests and multiple branching endings
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Race against inevitable global catastrophe (Lavos) while discovering character backstories and moral consequences of time travel
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assemble a legendary team across time periods, master collaborative magic techniques, and rewrite history to prevent apocalypse
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Revolutionary integration of multiple endings tied to player choices and timing; visible enemies on field maps instead of random encounters; character-focused narrative progression through side-quests rather than mandatory story paths; collaborative dual/triple tech system emphasizing party synergy over individual power

difficulty.lives_system: Save points allow restoration after party wipe; specific story-related battles allow or force player loss without penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Chapter-based save system with player-controlled save points throughout world
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Visible enemies allow player to choose engagement; character recruitment and equipment purchasing provide scaling difficulty options; multiple endings accessible regardless of combat difficulty

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment (weapons, armor, helmets, accessories) purchased in shops or found in treasure chests; stat-based progression through leveling; Techs unlock through character development and progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Time period access gates progression; character recruitment through story events; Tech mastery through gameplay
replayability_hooks: Multiple branching endings dependent on player choice and timing
Character-development side quests offering narrative depth
New Game+ accessibility (implied by ending variations)
Character recruitment order and party composition variations

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling across six time periods (prehistory to post-apocalypse)
NPC dialogue revealing cultural context of each era
Character backstory integration through optional side-quests
Visual design emphasizing temporal aesthetic differences

boss_design.philosophy: Lavos serves as recurring narrative threat with multiple manifestations; boss encounters integrated into character arcs and plot progression
notable_bosses: Lavos (primary antagonist with multiple encounter phases)
Character-specific confrontations related to personal storylines

environmental_storytelling: Ruins of advanced civilizations revealing rise-and-fall patterns
Contrast between pristine past and post-apocalyptic future
Architecture and technology evolution across eras
NPC settlements showing period-specific culture and governance
